title: The Library System
text_zh: 我常去的图书馆有一套安静但高效的系统。进门以后先刷身份证，门禁会发出很轻的一声「滴」。一楼是报纸和杂志区，灯光比较亮，可以说话但不能太大声。二楼是借阅区，按类别分好了，每排书架都有编号，找书很方便。三楼是自习区，绝对不能说话，连翻书的声音都尽量放轻。每层楼都有一个管理员，他们走路的时候鞋子不发出声音，像在水面上滑过一样。借书的流程也很简单：把书拿到一楼的自助机器上，扫一下身份证，再扫一下书的条形码，就可以带走了。还书也一样方便，门口有一个还书口，书塞进去就行。我特别喜欢这个图书馆的一个细节——每个座位旁边都有一个小灯，亮度刚好够看书，不会影响到旁边的人。这就是好系统的特点：每一个细节都是为了让人舒服。
text_pinyin: Wǒ cháng qù de túshūguǎn yǒu yí tào ānjìng dàn gāoxiào de xìtǒng. Jìn mén yǐhòu xiān shuā shēnfènzhèng, ménjìn huì fāchū hěn qīng de yì shēng「dī」. Yī lóu shì bàozhǐ hé zázhì qū, dēngguāng bǐjiào liàng, kěyǐ shuōhuà dàn bù néng tài dà shēng. Èr lóu shì jièyuè qū, àn lèibié fēn hǎo le, měi pái shūjià dōu yǒu biānhào, zhǎo shū hěn fāngbiàn. Sān lóu shì zìxí qū, juéduì bù néng shuōhuà, lián fān shū de shēngyīn dōu jǐnliàng fàng qīng. Měi céng lóu dōu yǒu yí gè guǎnlǐyuán, tāmen zǒulù de shíhou xiézi bù fāchū shēngyīn, xiàng zài shuǐ miàn shàng huá guò yíyàng. Jiè shū de liúchéng yě hěn jiǎndān: bǎ shū ná dào yī lóu de zìzhù jīqì shàng, sǎo yíxià shēnfènzhèng, zài sǎo yíxià shū de tiáoxíng mǎ, jiù kěyǐ dài zǒu le. Huán shū yě yíyàng fāngbiàn, ménkǒu yǒu yí gè huán shū kǒu, shū sāi jìnqù jiù xíng. Wǒ tèbié xǐhuan zhège túshūguǎn de yí gè xìjié — měi gè zuòwèi pángbiān dōu yǒu yí gè xiǎo dēng, liàngdù gānghǎo gòu kàn shū, bú huì yǐngxiǎng dào pángbiān de rén. Zhè jiù shì hǎo xìtǒng de tèdiǎn: měi yí gè xìjié dōu shì wèile ràng rén shūfu.
text_en: The library I often visit has a quiet but efficient system. Upon entering you swipe your ID card, the turnstile emitting a soft 'beep.' The first floor is the newspaper and magazine area — brighter lights, talking allowed but not too loud. The second floor is the borrowing area, organized by category, each shelf row numbered, making books easy to find. The third floor is the study area — absolutely no talking, even page-turning kept as quiet as possible. Each floor has an administrator who walks with shoes that make no sound, as if gliding over water. The borrowing process is simple: bring books to the first-floor self-service machine, scan your ID, scan the book's barcode, and take it home. Returns are equally convenient — a return slot by the entrance where you just slide books in. I especially love one detail about this library: every seat has a small lamp beside it, just bright enough to read by without disturbing the person next to you. That's the hallmark of a good system: every detail designed for comfort.

questions.q_zh: 三楼自习区的规则是什么？
questions.q_en: What is the rule for the third-floor study area?

questions.q_zh: 作者特别喜欢图书馆的什么细节？
questions.q_en: What detail does the author especially like about the library?

options.text: 每个座位旁边有一个小灯
options.pinyin: měi gè zuòwèi pángbiān yǒu yí gè xiǎo dēng
options.text_en: A small lamp beside every seat

title: The Hospital Waiting Room
text_zh: 去医院看病是一件需要耐心的事。挂号以后你会拿到一个号码，然后就开始等。候诊室的墙上有一个电子屏幕，显示现在叫到几号了。你可以算出前面大概还有多少人，每个人大概需要多长时间。我一般会带一本书，因为等两个小时是很正常的。候诊室的椅子是蓝色的塑料椅子，坐久了不太舒服。旁边的人在刷手机，有人在打电话，有小孩因为害怕哭了。护士穿着白色的衣服来来去去，走得很快，说话简洁有力。整个医院就像一台大机器，每个人都是其中的一个零件，各自做着自己的事情。虽然等待的过程很无聊，但我注意到了一件事：当屏幕上出现你的号码的时候，那一刻的感觉就像中了彩票一样高兴。也许等待本身就是一种训练，训练你学会在什么都不能做的时候保持平静。
text_pinyin: Qù yīyuàn kànbìng shì yí jiàn xūyào nàixīn de shì. Guàhào yǐhòu nǐ huì ná dào yí gè hàomǎ, ránhòu jiù kāishǐ děng. Hòuzhěn shì de qiáng shàng yǒu yí gè diànzǐ píngmù, xiǎnshì xiànzài jiào dào jǐ hào le. Nǐ kěyǐ suàn chū qiánmiàn dàgài hái yǒu duōshǎo rén, měi gè rén dàgài xūyào duō cháng shíjiān. Wǒ yìbān huì dài yì běn shū, yīnwèi děng liǎng gè xiǎoshí shì hěn zhèngcháng de. Hòuzhěn shì de yǐzi shì lánsè de sùliào yǐzi, zuò jiǔ le bú tài shūfu. Pángbiān de rén zài shuā shǒujī, yǒu rén zài dǎ diànhuà, yǒu xiǎohái yīnwèi hàipà kū le. Hùshi chuān zhe báisè de yīfu lái lái qù qù, zǒu de hěn kuài, shuōhuà jiǎnjié yǒulì. Zhěnggè yīyuàn jiù xiàng yì tái dà jīqì, měi gè rén dōu shì qízhōng de yí gè língjiàn, gèzì zuò zhe zìjǐ de shìqíng. Suīrán děngdài de guòchéng hěn wúliáo, dàn wǒ zhùyì dào le yí jiàn shì: dāng píngmù shàng chūxiàn nǐ de hàomǎ de shíhou, nà yí kè de gǎnjué jiù xiàng zhòng le cǎipiào yíyàng gāoxìng. Yěxǔ děngdài běnshēn jiù shì yì zhǒng xùnliàn, xùnliàn nǐ xuéhuì zài shénme dōu bù néng zuò de shíhou bǎochí píngjìng.
text_en: Going to the hospital requires patience. After registering you get a number, then start waiting. The waiting room wall has an electronic screen showing the current number being called. You can estimate how many people are ahead and how long each takes. I usually bring a book since waiting two hours is normal. The waiting room has blue plastic chairs — uncomfortable after sitting long. People nearby scroll their phones, someone makes a call, a child cries from fear. Nurses in white come and go quickly, speaking concisely and firmly. The entire hospital is like a big machine, each person a component, doing their own job. Though waiting is boring, I noticed something: when your number appears on the screen, that moment feels as happy as winning the lottery. Maybe waiting itself is a kind of training — training you to stay calm when you can't do anything.

questions.q_zh: 作者去医院一般带什么？
questions.q_en: What does the author usually bring to the hospital?
What does the author compare the hospital to?

title: The Neighborhood Committee
text_zh: 我们小区的居委会办公室在一楼，门上贴着办公时间和联系电话。里面只有三个工作人员，但管的事情很多：水电费的问题、邻居之间的纠纷、老人的关怀服务、垃圾分类的宣传……每天都有人来找他们。王阿姨是居委会的负责人，五十多岁，说话声音很大，但让人觉得很可靠。上周我家的水管坏了，我不知道该找谁来修。打电话给物业等了半天没人接。我走到居委会问了一下，王阿姨马上从抽屉里翻出一个本子，上面记满了各种维修师傅的电话号码。她帮我找到了一个水管工，还打电话帮我约好了时间。走的时候她说「修好了告诉我一声，要是没修好我再找别人」。这种基层的系统也许不那么高科技，但它有一种人情味。王阿姨的本子就是最好的数据库，而她的热情就是最好的算法。
text_pinyin: Wǒmen xiǎoqū de jūwěihuì bàngōngshì zài yī lóu, mén shàng tiē zhe bàngōng shíjiān hé liánxì diànhuà. Lǐmiàn zhǐ yǒu sān gè gōngzuò rényuán, dàn guǎn de shìqíng hěn duō: shuǐ diàn fèi de wèntí, línjū zhījiān de jiūfēn, lǎorén de guānhuái fúwù, lājī fēnlèi de xuānchuán……měi tiān dōu yǒu rén lái zhǎo tāmen. Wáng āyí shì jūwěihuì de fùzé rén, wǔshí duō suì, shuōhuà shēngyīn hěn dà, dàn ràng rén juéde hěn kěkào. Shàng zhōu wǒ jiā de shuǐguǎn huài le, wǒ bù zhīdào gāi zhǎo shéi lái xiū. Dǎ diànhuà gěi wùyè děng le bàntiān méi rén jiē. Wǒ zǒu dào jūwěihuì wèn le yíxià, Wáng āyí mǎshàng cóng chōuti lǐ fān chū yí gè běnzi, shàngmiàn jì mǎn le gè zhǒng wéixiū shīfu de diànhuà hàomǎ. Tā bāng wǒ zhǎo dào le yí gè shuǐguǎn gōng, hái dǎ diànhuà bāng wǒ yuē hǎo le shíjiān. Zǒu de shíhou tā shuō「xiū hǎo le gàosu wǒ yì shēng, yàoshi méi xiū hǎo wǒ zài zhǎo biéren」. Zhè zhǒng jīcéng de xìtǒng yěxǔ bù nàme gāo kējì, dàn tā yǒu yì zhǒng rénqíng wèi. Wáng āyí de běnzi jiù shì zuì hǎo de shùjùkù, ér tā de rèqíng jiù shì zuì hǎo de suànfǎ.
text_en: Our compound's neighborhood committee office is on the first floor, with office hours and phone numbers posted on the door. Only three staff members inside, but they manage many things: utility bill issues, neighbor disputes, elderly care services, recycling education — people come every day. Auntie Wang is the head, in her fifties, speaks loudly but feels very reliable. Last week my water pipe broke and I didn't know who to call. I phoned property management and waited a long time with no answer. I walked to the committee office and asked; Auntie Wang immediately pulled a notebook from her drawer, filled with phone numbers of various repair workers. She found me a plumber and even called to schedule a time for me. When I left she said 'let me know once it's fixed, and if it's not I'll find someone else.' This kind of grassroots system may not be high-tech, but it has human warmth. Auntie Wang's notebook is the best database, and her enthusiasm is the best algorithm.

questions.q_zh: 王阿姨怎么帮作者解决水管的问题？
questions.q_en: How did Auntie Wang help the author with the pipe problem?

options.text: 从本子里找到水管工的电话并帮忙约时间
options.pinyin: cóng běnzi lǐ zhǎo dào shuǐguǎn gōng de diànhuà bìng bāngmáng yuē shíjiān
options.text_en: Found a plumber's number from her notebook and scheduled a time
Told the author to call property management

questions.q_zh: 作者把王阿姨的本子比作什么？
questions.q_en: What does the author compare Auntie Wang's notebook to?

title: The School Cafeteria
text_zh: 大学食堂是一个非常有趣的小社会。每天中午十一点半左右，所有人同时涌向食堂，像一条河流入大海。一楼是最便宜的窗口，菜的种类多但排队也最长。二楼有面条、饺子和麻辣烫，价格稍微贵一点。三楼是小炒窗口，可以现点现做，等的时间最长但味道最好。每个人都有自己的策略：有人十一点就去，避开人群；有人先去最远的窗口，因为那里排队的人少；有人每天固定吃一个窗口的饭，和打饭的阿姨已经很熟了，阿姨看到他就知道他要什么。我的策略是先看一圈哪个队最短就排哪个。四年下来，我发现食堂不只是吃饭的地方——你在那里认识了新朋友，准备了考试，讨论了人生的大问题，也独自吃过很多顿安静的午饭。毕业以后最怀念的不是哪道菜，而是那种排着队和朋友聊天的普通日子。
text_pinyin: Dàxué shítáng shì yí gè fēicháng yǒuqù de xiǎo shèhuì. Měi tiān zhōngwǔ shíyī diǎn bàn zuǒyòu, suǒyǒu rén tóngshí yǒng xiàng shítáng, xiàng yì tiáo hé liú rù dàhǎi. Yī lóu shì zuì piányi de chuāngkǒu, cài de zhǒnglèi duō dàn páiduì yě zuì cháng. Èr lóu yǒu miàntiáo, jiǎozi hé málà tàng, jiàgé shāowēi guì yìdiǎn. Sān lóu shì xiǎo chǎo chuāngkǒu, kěyǐ xiàn diǎn xiàn zuò, děng de shíjiān zuì cháng dàn wèidào zuì hǎo. Měi gè rén dōu yǒu zìjǐ de cèlüè: yǒu rén shíyī diǎn jiù qù, bìkāi rénqún; yǒu rén xiān qù zuì yuǎn de chuāngkǒu, yīnwèi nàlǐ páiduì de rén shǎo; yǒu rén měi tiān gùdìng chī yí gè chuāngkǒu de fàn, hé dǎ fàn de āyí yǐjīng hěn shú le, āyí kàn dào tā jiù zhīdào tā yào shénme. Wǒ de cèlüè shì xiān kàn yí quān nǎge duì zuì duǎn jiù pái nǎge. Sì nián xiàlái, wǒ fāxiàn shítáng bù zhǐ shì chīfàn de dìfāng — nǐ zài nàlǐ rènshi le xīn péngyou, zhǔnbèi le kǎoshì, tǎolùn le rénshēng de dà wèntí, yě dúzì chī guò hěn duō dùn ānjìng de wǔfàn. Bìyè yǐhòu zuì huáiniàn de bú shì nǎ dào cài, ér shì nà zhǒng pái zhe duì hé péngyou liáo tiān de pǔtōng rìzi.
text_en: A university cafeteria is a fascinating small society. Every day around 11:30, everyone surges toward the cafeteria like a river flowing into the sea. The first floor has the cheapest windows — many dishes but the longest lines. The second floor has noodles, dumplings, and malatang, slightly pricier. The third floor is the stir-fry counter where dishes are made to order — longest wait but best taste. Everyone has their strategy: some go at eleven to avoid crowds; some head to the farthest window since fewer people line up there; some eat at the same counter every day, so familiar with the serving auntie that she knows their order on sight. My strategy was to scan which line was shortest and join that one. Over four years, I found the cafeteria was more than a place to eat — you made new friends there, studied for exams, discussed life's big questions, and also ate many quiet lunches alone. After graduation what I miss most isn't any particular dish, but those ordinary days of chatting with friends while standing in line.

questions.q_zh: 三楼的窗口有什么特点？
questions.q_en: What's special about the third floor counter?

options.text: 可以现点现做，味道最好但等最久
options.pinyin: kěyǐ xiàn diǎn xiàn zuò, wèidào zuì hǎo dàn děng zuì jiǔ
options.text_en: Made to order, best taste but longest wait

questions.q_zh: 毕业以后作者最怀念食堂的什么？
questions.q_en: What does the author miss most about the cafeteria after graduation?

options.text: 排着队和朋友聊天的普通日子
options.pinyin: pái zhe duì hé péngyou liáo tiān de pǔtōng rìzi
options.text_en: Ordinary days chatting with friends in line

title: The Express Delivery Network
text_zh: 中国的快递系统可能是世界上最高效的之一。我在网上买了一件衣服，从广州发出来，三天就到了北京。包裹从卖家的仓库开始，先送到广州的分拣中心，在那里被扫描、分类，然后装上卡车。到了中转站又被重新分拣，送上了去北京的货车。到北京以后先去大的分拣中心，再送到我家附近的快递站。快递员骑着电动三轮车把包裹送到我的小区，如果我不在家就放在快递柜里。整个过程中间经过了至少六七个环节，每个环节都有人在工作，但我只需要在手机上点一下「确认收货」就行了。我的快递员姓李，三十多岁，每天送两三百个包裹。他总是很忙，但看到我会说一句「今天有您的包裹」。有一次下大雨他还是准时送到了，包裹外面裹了一层塑料袋防水。这种细节没有人要求他做，但他做了。系统的背后是一个个具体的人。
text_pinyin: Zhōngguó de kuàidì xìtǒng kěnéng shì shìjiè shàng zuì gāoxiào de zhī yī. Wǒ zài wǎng shàng mǎi le yí jiàn yīfu, cóng Guǎngzhōu fā chūlái, sān tiān jiù dào le Běijīng. Bāoguǒ cóng màijiā de cāngkù kāishǐ, xiān sòng dào Guǎngzhōu de fēnjiǎn zhōngxīn, zài nàlǐ bèi sǎomiáo, fēnlèi, ránhòu zhuāng shàng kǎchē. Dào le zhōngzhuǎn zhàn yòu bèi chóngxīn fēnjiǎn, sòng shàng le qù Běijīng de huòchē. Dào Běijīng yǐhòu xiān qù dà de fēnjiǎn zhōngxīn, zài sòng dào wǒ jiā fùjìn de kuàidì zhàn. Kuàidì yuán qí zhe diàndòng sānlún chē bǎ bāoguǒ sòng dào wǒ de xiǎoqū, rúguǒ wǒ bú zài jiā jiù fàng zài kuàidì guì lǐ. Zhěnggè guòchéng zhōngjiān jīngguò le zhìshǎo liù qī gè huánjié, měi gè huánjié dōu yǒu rén zài gōngzuò, dàn wǒ zhǐ xūyào zài shǒujī shàng diǎn yíxià「quèrèn shōuhuò」jiù xíng le. Wǒ de kuàidì yuán xìng Lǐ, sānshí duō suì, měi tiān sòng liǎng sān bǎi gè bāoguǒ. Tā zǒng shì hěn máng, dàn kàn dào wǒ huì shuō yí jù「jīntiān yǒu nín de bāoguǒ」. Yǒu yí cì xià dà yǔ tā háishi zhǔnshí sòng dào le, bāoguǒ wàimiàn guǒ le yì céng sùliào dài fángshuǐ. Zhè zhǒng xìjié méiyǒu rén yāoqiú tā zuò, dàn tā zuò le. Xìtǒng de bèihòu shì yí gè gè jùtǐ de rén.
text_en: China's delivery system is probably one of the most efficient in the world. I bought a piece of clothing online, shipped from Guangzhou, and it arrived in Beijing in three days. The package starts at the seller's warehouse, goes to a Guangzhou sorting center where it's scanned and categorized, then loaded onto a truck. At a transit station it's re-sorted and put on a truck to Beijing. In Beijing it goes to a large sorting center, then to the delivery station near my home. The courier rides an electric tricycle to deliver packages to my compound; if I'm not home, it goes in the delivery locker. The whole process passes through at least six or seven stages, each with people working, but I only need to tap 'confirm receipt' on my phone. My courier is surnamed Li, in his thirties, delivering two to three hundred packages daily. He's always busy, but when he sees me he says 'got a package for you today.' Once during heavy rain he still delivered on time, the package wrapped in an extra plastic bag for waterproofing. Nobody required this detail, but he did it. Behind every system are individual people.

questions.q_zh: 包裹从广州到北京经过了几个环节？
questions.q_en: How many stages does the package go through from Guangzhou to Beijing?

questions.q_zh: 下雨天快递员做了什么特别的事？
questions.q_en: What special thing did the courier do on the rainy day?

options.text: 用塑料袋把包裹包起来防水
options.pinyin: yòng sùliào dài bǎ bāoguǒ bāo qǐlái fángshuǐ
options.text_en: Wrapped the package in plastic for waterproofing

options.pinyin: dǎ diànhuà ràng zuòzhě qù qǔ
options.text_en: Called the author to pick it up

title: The Subway Ecosystem
text_zh: 每天坐地铁上下班的人形成了一种独特的生态系统。早上七点到九点是高峰期，车厢里挤得几乎无法动弹。每个人都有一套自己的生存策略：有人总是站在车门旁边的角落里，靠着墙不容易被挤；有人知道哪一节车厢人最少，每次都走到那个位置等车；有人已经算好了到站以后上楼梯最快的车门是哪个。车厢里虽然很挤，但大家都保持着一种默契——背包背在前面，不占太多空间；手机声音关掉或者戴耳机；到站了要下车的时候说一声「让一让」大家就会让出一条路。地铁还有一种特殊的时间感。因为在地下，没有天空可以看，也不知道外面是晴天还是下雨。你只能通过站名来判断位置，通过播报来感知时间。每天在同样的时间、同样的线路、同样的车厢里，日子就这么一站一站地过去了。
text_pinyin: Měi tiān zuò dìtiě shàng xià bān de rén xíngchéng le yì zhǒng dútè de shēngtài xìtǒng. Zǎoshang qī diǎn dào jiǔ diǎn shì gāofēng qī, chēxiāng lǐ jǐ de jīhū wúfǎ dòngtán. Měi gè rén dōu yǒu yí tào zìjǐ de shēngcún cèlüè: yǒu rén zǒng shì zhàn zài chē mén pángbiān de jiǎoluò lǐ, kào zhe qiáng bù róngyì bèi jǐ; yǒu rén zhīdào nǎ yì jié chēxiāng rén zuì shǎo, měi cì dōu zǒu dào nàge wèizhì děng chē; yǒu rén yǐjīng suàn hǎo le dào zhàn yǐhòu shàng lóutī zuì kuài de chē mén shì nǎge. Chēxiāng lǐ suīrán hěn jǐ, dàn dàjiā dōu bǎochí zhe yì zhǒng mòqì — bēibāo bēi zài qiánmiàn, bù zhàn tài duō kōngjiān; shǒujī shēngyīn guān diào huòzhě dài ěrjī; dào zhàn le yào xià chē de shíhou shuō yì shēng「ràng yí ràng」dàjiā jiù huì ràng chū yì tiáo lù. Dìtiě hái yǒu yì zhǒng tèshū de shíjiān gǎn. Yīnwèi zài dìxià, méiyǒu tiānkōng kěyǐ kàn, yě bù zhīdào wàimiàn shì qíngtiān háishi xià yǔ. Nǐ zhǐ néng tōngguò zhàn míng lái pànduàn wèizhì, tōngguò bōbào lái gǎnzhī shíjiān. Měi tiān zài tóngyàng de shíjiān, tóngyàng de xiànlù, tóngyàng de chēxiāng lǐ, rìzi jiù zhème yí zhàn yí zhàn de guòqù le.
text_en: People who commute by subway daily form a unique ecosystem. Between 7 and 9 AM is rush hour — cars so packed you can barely move. Everyone has survival strategies: some always stand in the corner by the door, leaning against the wall to avoid being squeezed; some know which car has fewest people and always walk to that spot; some have calculated which door leads to the fastest stairway exit. Though the cars are crowded, everyone maintains an unspoken agreement — backpacks worn in front to save space, phone sounds off or earbuds in, and when someone needs to exit they say 'excuse me' and a path opens up. The subway also has a peculiar sense of time. Being underground, there's no sky to see, no way to know if it's sunny or raining outside. You can only judge location by station names and sense time through announcements. Every day at the same time, same line, same car — days pass station by station.

questions.q_zh: 高峰期车厢里大家有什么默契？
questions.q_en: What unspoken agreements do people keep during rush hour?

options.text: 背包背在前面，手机静音
options.pinyin: bēibāo bēi zài qiánmiàn, shǒujī jìngyīn
měi gè rén zhàn zài gùdìng wèizhì
options.text_en: Everyone stands in a fixed spot

questions.q_zh: 地铁里的时间感有什么特别？
questions.q_en: What's special about the sense of time in the subway?

options.text: 看不到天空，只能通过站名和播报感知时间
options.pinyin: kàn bú dào tiānkōng, zhǐ néng tōngguò zhàn míng hé bōbào gǎnzhī shíjiān
options.text_en: Can't see the sky, can only sense time through station names and announcements

options.text: 每一站停的时间都不一样
options.pinyin: měi yí zhàn tíng de shíjiān dōu bù yíyàng
options.text_en: Each stop lasts a different time
There's a large clock to check

title: The Night Shift
text_zh: 便利店的夜班从晚上十一点开始，到早上七点结束。我的朋友小陈在一家便利店做兼职夜班已经两年了。他告诉我，夜班有自己的节奏。十一点到十二点是最忙的时候，下班的人会来买零食和啤酒。十二点以后人就少了，偶尔有人来买烟或者泡面。凌晨两三点是最安静的时候，有时候一个小时都没有客人。这个时候小陈要做的事情是：检查商品的保质期、整理货架、擦地、补货。他说最喜欢补货的过程，把一箱箱的东西拆开，按照顺序整整齐齐地摆好，有一种说不出来的满足感。凌晨四点以后开始有上早班的人来买早餐和咖啡。六点左右环卫工人会进来买热水或者包子。小陈说他已经认识了很多夜班的常客，虽然大家很少说话，但彼此点个头就够了。城市在白天是属于所有人的，但在夜里，它属于这些安静地维持着运转的人。
text_pinyin: Biànlì diàn de yèbān cóng wǎnshang shíyī diǎn kāishǐ, dào zǎoshang qī diǎn jiéshù. Wǒ de péngyou Xiǎo Chén zài yì jiā biànlì diàn zuò jiānzhí yèbān yǐjīng liǎng nián le. Tā gàosu wǒ, yèbān yǒu zìjǐ de jiézòu. Shíyī diǎn dào shí'èr diǎn shì zuì máng de shíhou, xiàbān de rén huì lái mǎi língshí hé píjiǔ. Shí'èr diǎn yǐhòu rén jiù shǎo le, ǒu'ěr yǒu rén lái mǎi yān huòzhě pàomiàn. Língchén liǎng sān diǎn shì zuì ānjìng de shíhou, yǒu shíhou yí gè xiǎoshí dōu méiyǒu kèrén. Zhège shíhou Xiǎo Chén yào zuò de shìqíng shì: jiǎnchá shāngpǐn de bǎozhìqī, zhěnglǐ huòjià, cā dì, bǔ huò. Tā shuō zuì xǐhuan bǔ huò de guòchéng, bǎ yì xiāng xiāng de dōngxi chāi kāi, ànzhào shùnxù zhěngzhěng qíqí de bǎi hǎo, yǒu yì zhǒng shuō bu chūlái de mǎnzú gǎn. Língchén sì diǎn yǐhòu kāishǐ yǒu shàng zǎobān de rén lái mǎi zǎocān hé kāfēi. Liù diǎn zuǒyòu huánwèi gōngrén huì jìnlái mǎi rè shuǐ huòzhě bāozi. Xiǎo Chén shuō tā yǐjīng rènshi le hěn duō yèbān de chángkè, suīrán dàjiā hěn shǎo shuōhuà, dàn bǐcǐ diǎn gè tóu jiù gòu le. Chéngshì zài báitiān shì shǔyú suǒyǒu rén de, dàn zài yèlǐ, tā shǔyú zhèxiē ānjìng de wéichí zhe yùnzhuǎn de rén.
text_en: The convenience store night shift starts at 11 PM and ends at 7 AM. My friend Xiao Chen has been working part-time night shifts at a convenience store for two years. He told me night shifts have their own rhythm. 11 PM to midnight is busiest — people getting off work buy snacks and beer. After midnight it's quieter, the occasional person buying cigarettes or instant noodles. 2-3 AM is the quietest — sometimes an hour without a single customer. During this time Xiao Chen checks expiration dates, organizes shelves, mops the floor, and restocks. He says he likes restocking best — opening boxes one by one, arranging items neatly in order, with an indescribable sense of satisfaction. After 4 AM, early shift workers start coming for breakfast and coffee. Around 6, sanitation workers come in for hot water or steamed buns. Xiao Chen says he's come to know many night-shift regulars. Though they rarely talk, a nod to each other is enough. The city during the day belongs to everyone, but at night it belongs to these people who quietly keep things running.

questions.q_zh: 小陈最喜欢夜班的哪个工作？
questions.q_en: What's Xiao Chen's favorite night shift task?

questions.q_zh: 凌晨六点左右谁会来便利店？
questions.q_en: Who comes to the convenience store around 6 AM?

title: The Exam Hall
text_zh: 考试那天，所有人提前半小时到了考场门口。每个人手里拿着准考证和身份证，表情各不相同。有人在低声背单词，有人闭着眼睛深呼吸，有人在和旁边的人小声讨论某个知识点。考场的门准时打开，监考老师站在门口检查证件。进去以后，每张桌子上已经放好了答题卡和草稿纸。桌子之间的距离刚好够大，让你看不到别人的卷子。铃声响了以后，整个教室突然安静下来，只有翻卷子的声音和笔在纸上写字的声音。时间一分一秒地过去，你能感觉到空气中的紧张感。中间有人上厕所，监考老师陪着去，每一步都在制度的规定之内。考试结束的铃声响起来的时候，有人长出了一口气，有人脸色发白。走出考场，外面的阳光让人觉得恍惚，好像刚从另一个世界回来。但无论结果怎样，这一切都结束了，这本身就是一种解脱。
text_pinyin: Kǎoshì nà tiān, suǒyǒu rén tíqián bàn xiǎoshí dào le kǎochǎng ménkǒu. Měi gè rén shǒu lǐ ná zhe zhǔn kǎo zhèng hé shēnfènzhèng, biǎoqíng gè bù xiāngtóng. Yǒu rén zài dī shēng bèi dāncí, yǒu rén bì zhe yǎnjīng shēn hūxī, yǒu rén zài hé pángbiān de rén xiǎo shēng tǎolùn mǒu gè zhīshi diǎn. Kǎochǎng de mén zhǔnshí dǎkāi, jiānkǎo lǎoshī zhàn zài ménkǒu jiǎnchá zhèngjiàn. Jìnqù yǐhòu, měi zhāng zhuōzi shàng yǐjīng fàng hǎo le dátí kǎ hé cǎogǎo zhǐ. Zhuōzi zhījiān de jùlí gānghǎo gòu dà, ràng nǐ kàn bú dào biéren de juǎnzi. Líng shēng xiǎng le yǐhòu, zhěnggè jiàoshì tūrán ānjìng xiàlái, zhǐ yǒu fān juǎnzi de shēngyīn hé bǐ zài zhǐ shàng xiě zì de shēngyīn. Shíjiān yì fēn yì miǎo de guòqù, nǐ néng gǎnjué dào kōngqì zhōng de jǐnzhāng gǎn. Zhōngjiān yǒu rén shàng cèsuǒ, jiānkǎo lǎoshī péi zhe qù, měi yí bù dōu zài zhìdù de guīdìng zhī nèi. Kǎoshì jiéshù de líng shēng xiǎng qǐlái de shíhou, yǒu rén cháng chū le yì kǒu qì, yǒu rén liǎnsè fā bái. Zǒu chū kǎochǎng, wàimiàn de yángguāng ràng rén juéde huǎnghū, hǎoxiàng gāng cóng lìng yí gè shìjiè huílái. Dàn wúlùn jiéguǒ zěnyàng, zhè yíqiè dōu jiéshù le, zhè běnshēn jiù shì yì zhǒng jiětuō.
text_en: On exam day, everyone arrived half an hour early at the exam hall entrance. Each person held their admission ticket and ID, expressions all different. Some murmured vocabulary, some breathed deeply with eyes closed, some quietly discussed a topic with someone nearby. The hall doors opened on time, the proctor standing at the entrance checking documents. Inside, each desk already had an answer sheet and scratch paper. The distance between desks was just enough that you couldn't see others' papers. After the bell, the entire classroom fell suddenly silent — only the sound of turning pages and pens writing on paper. Time passed second by second; you could feel the tension in the air. When someone needed the restroom, the proctor accompanied them — every step within the rules. When the ending bell rang, some exhaled deeply, some went pale. Walking out, the sunlight outside felt dizzying, as if returning from another world. But regardless of the result, it was all over, and that itself was a kind of relief.

questions.q_zh: 铃声响了以后教室里有什么声音？
questions.q_en: What sounds were in the classroom after the bell?

options.pinyin: fān juǎnzi hé xiě zì de shēngyīn

questions.q_zh: 走出考场以后作者什么感觉？
questions.q_en: How did the author feel after leaving the exam hall?

options.text: 觉得恍惚，好像从另一个世界回来
options.pinyin: juéde huǎnghū, hǎoxiàng cóng lìng yí gè shìjiè huílái
options.text_en: Felt dazed, as if returning from another world

options.pinyin: mǎshàng xiǎng kǎo xià yì chǎng
options.text_en: Immediately wanted to take the next exam
